function.name: brightdata-mcp_search_engine
function.description: Scrape search results from Google, Bing or Yandex. Returns SERP results in markdown (URL, title, description)

training_data.question: Perform an in-depth search on Google to identify the most relevant and authoritative top search results for "artificial intelligence trends," analyze how these results reflect current advancements and future directions in the field, and evaluate the credibility of the sources based on their publication date and author expertise.

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_search_engine
candidate_tools.description: Scrape search results from Google, Bing or Yandex. Returns SERP results in markdown (URL, title, description)

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_screenshot
candidate_tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page

full_page.description:
Whether to screenshot the full page (default: false)
You should avoid fullscreen if it's not important, since the images can be quite large

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_scroll
candidate_tools.description: Scroll to the bottom of the current page

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_navigate
candidate_tools.description: Navigate a scraping browser session to a new URL

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_extract
candidate_tools.description: Scrape a webpage and extract structured data as JSON. First scrapes the page as markdown, then uses AI sampling to convert it to structured JSON format. This tool can unlock any webpage even if it uses bot detection or CAPTCHA.

extraction_prompt.description: Custom prompt to guide the extraction process. If not provided, will extract general structured data from the page.

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_session_stats
candidate_tools.description: Tell the user about the tool usage during this session

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_get_text
candidate_tools.description: Get the text content of the current page

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_web_data_linkedin_people_search
candidate_tools.description:
Quickly read structured linkedin people search data
This can be a cache lookup, so it can be more reliable than scraping

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_links
candidate_tools.description:
Get all links on the current page, text and selectors
It's strongly recommended that you call the links tool to check that your click target is valid

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_click
candidate_tools.description:
Click on an element.
Avoid calling this unless you know the element selector (you can use other tools to find those)

selector.description: CSS selector for the element to click

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_extract
candidate_tools.description: Scrape a webpage and extract structured data as JSON. First scrapes the page as markdown, then uses AI sampling to convert it to structured JSON format. This tool can unlock any webpage even if it uses bot detection or CAPTCHA.

extraction_prompt.description: Custom prompt to guide the extraction process. If not provided, will extract general structured data from the page.

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_session_stats
candidate_tools.description: Tell the user about the tool usage during this session

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_get_text
candidate_tools.description: Get the text content of the current page

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_web_data_linkedin_people_search
candidate_tools.description:
Quickly read structured linkedin people search data
This can be a cache lookup, so it can be more reliable than scraping

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_links
candidate_tools.description:
Get all links on the current page, text and selectors
It's strongly recommended that you call the links tool to check that your click target is valid

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_scraping_browser_click
candidate_tools.description:
Click on an element.
Avoid calling this unless you know the element selector (you can use other tools to find those)

selector.description: CSS selector for the element to click

training_data.question: Simulate a user interaction by pressing the "Submit" button on the page using its CSS selector: #submit-button, ensuring the action is delayed by 2 seconds to mimic human behavior, and verify that the button's state changes to "disabled" after submission to confirm the interaction.

candidate_tools.name: brightdata-mcp_search_engine
candidate_tools.description: Scrape search results from Google, Bing or Yandex. Returns SERP results in markdown (URL, title, description)

candidate_tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page

full_page.description:
